title: Controlled solid-state crystallization with formamidinium acetate for fully vacuum-deposited perovskite solar cells - Nature Energy
gist: A Nanjing Tech University team improves fully vacuum-deposited (solvent-free) perovskite solar cells to a certified 25.53% power conversion efficiency by using formamidinium acetate instead of standard precursors, which lowers the crystallization barrier and passivates grain-boundary defects — narrowing the long-standing performance gap between scalable vacuum-deposited cells and higher-efficiency but harder-to-scale solution-processed devices, while also retaining over 95% efficiency after 1,000 hours of stability testing.

insights.claim: Vacuum deposition is a promising manufacturing route for perovskite solar cells because it is solvent-free and considered compatible with existing large-area semiconductor fabrication infrastructure, but vacuum-deposited devices have historically underperformed solution-processed perovskite cells.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Vacuum deposition is viewed in the literature as a 'short track to commercialization' for perovskite PV because it avoids solvent handling and toxicity issues at scale; closing its efficiency gap versus solution processing directly affects which manufacturing route becomes dominant for commercial perovskite and perovskite/silicon tandem lines.
insights.quote: Vacuum deposition is a promising route for the fabrication of perovskite solar cells (PSCs). However, vacuum-deposited PSCs have not yet matched the performance achieved by solution-processed devices.

insights.claim: The new fully vacuum-deposited device achieved a power conversion efficiency of 25.53% and an electroluminescence external quantum efficiency of 18.38%, a substantial step up that narrows the gap to top solution-processed and record perovskite cell efficiencies (in the mid-to-high 20% range).
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: A 25.53% certified efficiency from a solvent-free, scalable process is a concrete data point for evaluating whether vacuum-deposited perovskite manufacturing lines can now compete on performance with solution-based incumbents.
insights.quote: As a result, the PSCs achieve a power conversion efficiency of 25.53% and an electroluminescence external quantum efficiency of 18.38%.

themes.theme: Formamidinium acetate as the crystallization-control mechanism

insights.claim: Formamidinium acetate reacts directly with lead iodide (PbI2) to form FAPbI3 seed layers, substantially lowering the barrier for solid-state conversion of precursors first into the crystalline delta-phase and then into the desired alpha-phase perovskite structure.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Identifies a specific, patentable precursor-chemistry substitution (formamidinium acetate for standard formamidinium iodide) as the mechanism enabling higher-quality vacuum-deposited perovskite films, relevant IP to track for perovskite equipment/materials suppliers.
insights.quote: We find that formamidinium acetate reacts directly with PbI2 to form FAPbI3 seed layers, substantially lowering the barrier to the solid-state conversion of precursors into the crystalline δ-phase and their subsequent transformation into the α-phase perovskite.

insights.claim: Excess acetate from the formamidinium acetate precursor passivates grain-boundary defects in the perovskite film, suppressing non-radiative recombination losses that otherwise cap device voltage and efficiency.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: A single additive achieving both crystallization control and defect passivation simplifies the vacuum-deposition process recipe, a practically relevant simplification for manufacturing scale-up versus multi-step treatment protocols.
insights.quote: Moreover, the excess acetate passivates grain boundary defects, thereby suppressing non-radiative recombination losses.

themes.theme: Stability results support the solvent-free manufacturing case

insights.claim: The solvent-free vacuum-deposited devices retained over 95% of their initial power conversion efficiency after 1,000 hours under the ISOS-L-1 stability testing protocol, indicating the solvent-free fabrication route also confers strong operational stability.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Stability under standardized ISOS protocols is a key gating metric for commercialization and bankability of perovskite PV; a >95% retention at 1,000 hours strengthens the investment case for vacuum-deposition equipment and process IP as perovskite modules move toward warranty-backed commercial products.
insights.quote: the solvent-free fabrication also enables excellent device stability, with the devices retaining over 95% of their initial power conversion efficiency after 1,000 h under the ISOS-L-1 protocol

insights.claim: Cited literature frames vapor-phase (vacuum) deposition of perovskite photovoltaics as a potential 'short track to commercialization,' implying industry and academic consensus already views the solvent-free route as strategically important for scale-up, not merely a niche lab technique.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Signals that equipment vendors and cell makers investing in vacuum/vapor-deposition perovskite lines (versus slot-die/solution coating) are pursuing a route with growing technical and commercial credibility, relevant to capex allocation decisions in perovskite and perovskite-silicon tandem manufacturing.
